title: artocarpus reticulatus
intro: Artocarpus reticulatus, also known as the jackfruit tree, is a tropical evergreen tree native to parts of Asia and Africa, producing the largest tree-borne fruit in the world. The jackfruit tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of the jackfruit tree include its potential to aid in weight loss, lower cholesterol levels, and improve digestion, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. Various herbal preparations of the jackfruit tree are used to treat various ailments, including teas, tinctures, and infusions made from its leaves, bark, and fruit.
